schema.appDescription: Calculate real returns after adjusting for inflation. Find out the true purchasing power growth of your mutual fund or investment after inflation.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Inflation-Adjusted Returns Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the difference between nominal and real returns?
faq.a: Nominal return: the stated percentage gain on investment. Real return: gain after subtracting inflation. Formula: Real Return ≈ Nominal Return − Inflation (approximately). Exact: Real Return = (1 + Nominal Rate) ÷ (1 + Inflation Rate) − 1. Example: 12% nominal, 6% inflation → Real return = 5.66%.

faq.q: Which investments provide the best real returns in India?
faq.a: Historical real returns (nominal return minus 6% inflation): Equity MF (12–15% − 6% = 6–9% real), PPF (7.1% − 6% = 1.1% real), FD (7% − 6% = 1% real), gold (7–9% − 6% = 1–3% real), real estate (7–10% − 6% = 1–4% real). Equity provides the highest real return over long periods.

faq.q: Why do FDs not beat inflation effectively?
faq.a: Bank FD rates (6.5–7.5%) barely exceed inflation (6%). After-tax real return: FD at 7% in the 30% bracket = 4.9% post-tax, minus 6% inflation = −1.1% real return. FD holders are actually losing purchasing power in real terms after tax. FDs are appropriate only for 1–3 year goals.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹5 lakh SIP vs FD over 10 years (inflation-adjusted)
examples.text: ₹5 lakh in equity MF at 12% for 10 years: nominal ₹15.5 lakh, real value ₹8.7 lakh (at 6% inflation). FD at 7%: nominal ₹9.84 lakh, real value ₹5.5 lakh. Real gain: equity ₹3.7 lakh vs FD ₹0.5 lakh. Equity provides 7× more real wealth creation.

examples.heading: Gold investment — inflation hedge?
examples.text: ₹5 lakh in gold at 8% nominal return for 10 years: nominal ₹10.79 lakh. Real value at 6% inflation: ₹6.03 lakh. Real gain: ₹1.03 lakh on ₹5 lakh. Gold is a partial inflation hedge but doesn't generate income. Use as 5–10% portfolio diversifier, not as primary wealth builder.
